goal: Maximize validation accuracy on sklearn breast cancer dataset in five experiments.
hypothesis: Try conservative gradient boosting as a final ensemble baseline and compare prediction accuracy against actual metrics.

risks: Gradient boosting may overfit on small datasets like breast_cancer if not properly regularized
Prediction accuracy might still miss quantitative details as seen in previous runs
Performance may not significantly exceed existing SVC and logistic regression baselines
world_model_prediction.rationale: The gradient boosting model with conservative parameters (n_estimators=150, learning_rate=0.05) aligns with the world-model-guided policy to try a conservative gradient boosting ensemble baseline. Previous models achieved 0.986 accuracy with SVC and logistic regression with scaling, so gradient boosting should perform similarly or slightly better. The small dataset size and conservative hyperparameters reduce overfitting risks while maintaining reasonable runtime expectations.

comparison.lesson: Run succeeded, but the prediction missed at least one quantitative detail.

next_decision: Stop and report the best observed model.
